title: words for social exhaustion
caption: For when people feel heavier than usual. #vocabulary #selfawareness #communication #verbsy

words.definition: a general distrust or dislike of people.
words.example: Not introversion. More like your patience left the room.

words.definition: not motivated by social interaction.
words.example: Quiet by preference, not always by fear.

words.definition: tired from too much experience or disappointment.
words.example: When everything feels a little too predictable.

title: words for soft power
caption: Quiet influence has its own vocabulary. #socialintelligence #communication #wordchoice #verbsy

words.definition: seriousness and weight that earns respect.
words.example: The room gets quieter when they speak.

words.definition: skilled at saying the right thing without offense.
words.example: Honest, but not careless with it.

words.definition: careful and skillful in handling people.
words.example: They can disagree without making enemies.

words.definition: offense or resentment after feeling insulted.
words.example: The smile stays, but the mood changes.

words.definition: deep bitterness that lasts.
words.example: Anger after it has had time to harden.

words.definition: silently resentful or gloomy.
words.example: When the quiet is doing the arguing.

title: words for losing yourself
caption: For the moments when your inner life feels unfamiliar. #selfawareness #vocabulary #mindset #verbsy

words.definition: a feeling of distance from someone, something, or yourself.
words.example: You are there, but not fully with yourself.

words.definition: feeling isolated or disconnected.
words.example: The room is full, and still you feel far away.

words.definition: feeling detached from your thoughts, body, or surroundings.
words.example: Like watching your life from a few steps back.

title: words for subtle jealousy
caption: A precise word makes the feeling easier to catch. #emotionalintelligence #wordchoice #verbsy

words.definition: pain at someone else having what you want.
words.example: The compliment that costs you effort.

words.definition: strongly wanting what belongs to someone else.
words.example: Desire with a little sharp edge.

words.definition: to resent someone for having or receiving something.
words.example: You are happy for them, almost.

title: words that make you sound perceptive
caption: For when your point needs to land with precision. #vocabulary #wordchoice #communication #verbsy

words.definition: quick to notice and understand what matters.
words.example: An astute comment makes people pause.

words.definition: highly perceptive or observant.
words.example: You saw the pattern before it became obvious.

words.definition: sharp in insight or judgment.
words.example: Taste, but with a blade behind it.

words.definition: showing deep insight.
words.example: The kind of question that exposes the real issue.

words.definition: sharp, practical, and good at reading situations.
words.example: Smart with the lights on.

title: words for when the vibe shifts
caption: Some shifts are easier to understand once they have a name. #socialintelligence #vocabulary #verbsy

words.definition: a sudden thrill or shiver of feeling.
words.example: When the air changes before anyone says why.

words.definition: a strained feeling between people or ideas.
words.example: Nothing happened, but everyone noticed it.

words.definition: a sign that something important may happen.
words.example: The tiny moment before the story turns.

title: stop saying awkward
caption: Awkward has levels. These words are cleaner. #wordchoice #vocabulary #communication #verbsy

words.definition: moving or behaving in an awkward way.
words.example: Clumsy, but somehow specific.

words.definition: stiff, unnatural, or overly formal.
words.example: The conversation sounded like a script.

words.definition: socially or physically awkward.
words.example: A polished word for an unpolished moment.

words.definition: charming in a way that can mislead.
words.example: Attractive enough to lower your guard.

words.definition: subtly working into someone's favor or mind.
words.example: They never push. They seep.

words.definition: tempting or powerfully attractive.
words.example: Not just romantic. Ideas can be seductive too.

title: words for overexplaining yourself
caption: For when the explanation becomes the anxiety. #communication #selfawareness #wordchoice #verbsy

words.definition: using too many words to say something simple.
words.example: Talking around the point because the point feels risky.

words.definition: wordy, unfocused, or spread out.
words.example: The message had a map, but no destination.

words.definition: showing regret or a need to excuse yourself.
words.example: When every sentence asks permission to exist.
